subject: usb-storage: blacklist Realtek WiFi driver CD-ROM.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Realtek WiFi adapter (0x0bda:0x1a2b) would enumerate as a fake CD-ROM (mass storage device) and usb_modeswitch would fail to switch it to WiFi mode, leaving it non-functional.

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: sonnet-verified correction: usb_modeswitch is NOT part of the current Buildroot_MiSTer build. output/.config (regenerated 2026-07-14, matches configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig) explicitly shows both `# BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH is not set` and `# BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH_DATA is not set` (output/.config:1570-1571); output/target has zero usb_modeswitch files; `git log -S USB_MODESWITCH -- configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig` returns no history -- the option was never enabled via our defconfig. The files the original record cited (work/imgroot/usr/lib/udev/rules.d/40-usb_modeswitch.rules, work/imgroot/usr/share/usb_modeswitch/0bda:1a2b) are stale build-output leftovers (dated 2022-12-24 and 2025-04-02 respectively) from an unrelated/older build of the work/ tree, which is itself .gitignore'd and not tracked source. There is currently no userspace mechanism in our shipped image that provides fallback coverage for this device.

superseded_by: a3dc32c635bae0ae569f489e00de0e8f015bfc25 (vanilla usb-storage unusual_devs.h blacklist, equivalent mechanism)

notes:
MiSTer's 5.15 fork approach: adds 0x0bda:0x1a2b to the ignore_ids[] table in usual-tables.c (struct ignore_entry only captures vid/pid/bcdmin/bcdmax -- ANY match causes usb_usual_ignore_device() to return -ENXIO from storage_probe(), unconditional of any flags field) with narrow bcdDevice range (0x0000-0x9999). Vanilla 6.18.38 approach: adds an entry to unusual_devs.h (the MAIN usb_storage_usb_ids match table, a different table from ignore_ids) with the US_FL_IGNORE_DEVICE flag and wider bcdDevice range (0x0000-0xffff), AND also adds a companion entry for 0x0bda:0xa192 (Mercury MW310UH, confirmed present at unusual_devs.h:1520). This flag is checked separately in usb.c:689 (`if (us->fflags & US_FL_IGNORE_DEVICE) return -ENODEV;`), a genuinely different code path/table from the fork's ignore_ids mechanism (usb.c:1212) though both live in drivers/usb/storage/usb.c's storage_probe() and produce the same practical outcome: usb-storage refuses to bind the device. Equivalence='equivalent' (not byte-identical) is the right grade given the differing mechanism/table and the wider range plus extra device vanilla covers.

sonnet-verified correction (significant): the original record claimed 'kernel-level suppression is redundant given usb_modeswitch coverage' and cited userspace_coupling.coupled=true backed by files under work/imgroot/. Independent verification found this is WRONG for our actual build: BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH and BR2_PACKAGE_USB_MODESWITCH_DATA are both explicitly unset in the current output/.config, and never appear in configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig's git history. The work/imgroot files cited are stale artifacts (2022/2025 timestamps) from the gitignored work/ build-output tree, not the current shipped rootfs (output/target has zero usb_modeswitch files). Conclusion: there is NO userspace-level fallback for this device in Buildroot_MiSTer today. The kernel-level blacklist (currently obtained for free by building against vanilla 6.18.38, since a3dc32c635ba is upstream) is the SOLE protection mechanism -- not redundant, but load-bearing. This does not change the disposition (still dropped-upstream/equivalent, correctly not carried as a 00xx patch since vanilla covers it) but it does invalidate the 'redundant' framing and raises the practical importance of keeping the vanilla kernel source (not a fork patch) as the source of this protection going forward. Disposition re-verified against patch-provenance.md:345, which matches exactly (byte-for-byte) and does not itself make the redundancy claim -- that claim originated solely in this record's own (unverified) notes.

sonnet-verified: corrected
